# Item

Composes compact rows or cards from media, content, metadata, and actions.

## Installation

Add Item from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/item
```

## Usage

Use `ItemContent` for the flexible text column, `ItemMedia` for a leading icon or image, and `ItemActions` for controls that operate on that item. Header and footer regions span the full row when secondary metadata needs its own line.

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-md flex-col gap-4">
    <Item variant="outline">
      <ItemMedia variant="icon">
        <BadgeCheckIcon />
      </ItemMedia>
      <ItemContent>
        <ItemTitle>Profile verified</ItemTitle>
        <ItemDescription>Your account can now publish packages.</ItemDescription>
      </ItemContent>
      <ItemActions>
        <Button variant="outline" size="sm">View</Button>
      </ItemActions>
    </Item>

    <Item variant="muted" size="sm" as-child>
      <a href="#usage">
        <ItemContent>
          <ItemTitle>Read the usage guide</ItemTitle>
        </ItemContent>
        <ItemActions>
          <ChevronRightIcon />
        </ItemActions>
      </a>
    </Item>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { BadgeCheckIcon, ChevronRightIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Item,
  ItemActions,
  ItemContent,
  ItemDescription,
  ItemMedia,
  ItemTitle,
} from '@/Components/Item'
</script>
```

## Variants and size

The `default` variant blends into its container, `outline` draws an individual boundary, and `muted` provides a quiet filled surface. Use the `sm` size for dense lists and the default size when media, descriptions, or actions need more breathing room.

`ItemMedia` separately supports `default`, `icon`, and `image` presentation. Always keep image alternative text and do not rely on media alone to identify the row.

## Groups and separators

`ItemGroup` provides `role="list"`. Give each Item `role="listitem"` when using that grouping, or use native list markup when document semantics are more important than polymorphic composition. `ItemSeparator` adds a horizontal separator between adjacent items; avoid both outlines and separators when one boundary is enough.

## Interactive items

Use `as-child` when the entire item is one link so hover and focus styles land on the real anchor. Do not make the complete row a link when it also contains independent buttons, menus, checkboxes, or nested links. Read the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) before forwarding Item behavior through a custom component.

## Responsive layout

Items wrap when space is tight, while content keeps a zero minimum width so long text can clamp and wrap. Test rows with long translated titles and multiple actions. Move secondary controls into a menu or a full-width footer when they crowd the primary content.

## When to use

- Use Item for repeated resources, notifications, settings, or activity rows.
- Use Card for a larger self-contained region with header, body, and footer.
- Use Table when aligned columns and column headers are essential to comparison.

Hosted documentation: [Item](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/item)
